name: Aldea del Rey and Viso del Marqués from Santa Cruz de Mudela – circular via Almuradiel and Torrenueva
name_original: Aldea del Rey y Viso del Marqués desde Santa Cruz de Mudela – circular por Almuradiel y Torrenueva
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Santa Cruz De Mudela traverse a diverse landscape in Spain's Castilla-La Mancha region, bordering the Sierra Morena mountain range. The terrain features expansive plains, ancient oak trees, and pastures, transitioning into rugged mountainous areas with hills and unique rock formations. Cyclists can expect routes that include both moderate circuits through natural landscapes and more demanding paths with significant elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around Santa Cruz De Mudela The most popular road cycling route is Fresnedas River Gorges and Collado Pisa Loop, a 50.1 miles (80.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 41 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores varied topography including river gorges. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Almuradiel - Viso del Marqu, a moderate 43.7 miles (70.3 km) path. This route offers a journey through the natural landscapes of the Sierra Morena region. Local road cyclists also love the Castellar de Santiago from Almuradiel, a 51.0 miles (82.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Santa Cruz De Mudela is defined by rolling hills, expansive plains, and ancient oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate circuits to challenging routes with significant climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Cruz De Mudela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Santa Cruz De Mudela?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, from expansive plains and ancient oak trees in the Valle de Alcudia to rugged, mountainous areas with hills and unique rock formations in the Sierra Morena. You'll find a mix of moderate circuits through natural landscapes and more demanding paths with significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes for different skill levels?
A: Yes, the area caters to various abilities. You can find routes ranging from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085915474/almuradiel-viso-del-marqu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;moderate paths&lt;/a&gt; through natural landscapes to more challenging routes with significant climbs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1273156311/fresnedas-river-gorges-and-collado-pisa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fresnedas River Gorges and Collado Pisa Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on road cycling routes?
A: You can expect to cycle through varied natural features, including the 'dark lands' and dense vegetation of the Sierra Morena, expansive plains with ancient oak trees in the Valle de Alcudia, and areas with unique rock formations. Some routes also feature river gorges, adding to the scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several points of interest are accessible. You might pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6658045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bullring (Las Virtudes)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1400097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta del Vino Square&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5490390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Quixote Equestrian Statue (Valdepeñas)&lt;/a&gt;. Other historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6127563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Trinitarians&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5623641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Bernardo Convent&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santa Cruz De Mudela?
A: The climate in this part of Spain is generally favorable for cycling almost year-round. However, spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) are particularly ideal due to more moderate temperatures, making for a comfortable riding experience.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080398353/valdepenas-and-villanueva-de-los-infantes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdepeñas and Villanueva de los Infantes Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer circular ride, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085899729/aldea-del-rey-and-viso-del-marques-from-santa-cruz-de-mudela-circular-via-almuradiel-and-torrenueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldea del Rey and Viso del Marqués from Santa Cruz de Mudela – circular via Almuradiel and Torrenueva&lt;/a&gt; is another popular option.

Q: Can I find easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes?
A: While many routes offer moderate to difficult challenges, there are also easier options available. These typically traverse flatter sections of the plains, providing a less strenuous ride for beginners or those looking for a relaxed outing.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes in the area?
A: For longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080398353/valdepenas-and-villanueva-de-los-infantes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdepeñas and Villanueva de los Infantes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 128 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085899729/aldea-del-rey-and-viso-del-marques-from-santa-cruz-de-mudela-circular-via-almuradiel-and-torrenueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldea del Rey and Viso del Marqués from Santa Cruz de Mudela – circular via Almuradiel and Torrenueva&lt;/a&gt;, spanning around 120 km.

Q: Are there routes that explore river gorges?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1273156311/fresnedas-river-gorges-and-collado-pisa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fresnedas River Gorges and Collado Pisa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a notable route that specifically explores varied topography, including scenic river gorges, offering a unique cycling experience.

Q: What is the general elevation profile for road cycling in this region?
A: The elevation profile varies significantly. Routes can range from relatively flat sections across the plains to challenging climbs in the mountainous areas of the Sierra Morena, with some routes featuring over 1000 meters of elevation gain, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092434874/castellar-de-santiago-from-almuradiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de Santiago from Almuradiel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Santa Cruz De Mudela?
A: There are 10 road cycling routes detailed in this guide, offering a variety of distances and difficulties to explore the region's diverse landscapes.
tips:
This town is located in the valley of the Jabalón river, a tributary of the Guadiana on its left bank. In addition, its municipal term is part of the center of the province.

It is characterized by having a landscape with a great presence, especially in the cool mountains of the southwest with gall oaks, pine forests and streams, and in the west, in the area known as the valley, with the presence of rebollos, strawberry trees, heather. Therefore, we will find richness and natural diversity throughout its term.
In the urban nucleus is the Palacio de Clavería from the 16th century, home to the clavero, the person who guarded the keys to the Convent of Calatrava.

Other religious constructions are the Church of San Jorge Mártir and the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora del Valle.

In addition to the Palace of the Marqués de Santa Cruz, which is a Renaissance jewel built between 1564 and 1586 by the Spanish sailor Álvaro de Bazán, Viso del Marqués is home to other places of interest:


Church of Our Lady of the Assumption: This Gothic-Renaissance style church has a central nave offset from the presbytery, built on the remains of a previous church. On its walls, you will find the curious “Lagarto del Viso”, a stuffed crocodile brought from the Nile by the Marquis during one of his expeditions.


Plaza del Pradillo: The center of the town is surrounded by the Marqués Palace, the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption and the Tower of the old convent.


AVAN Natural Sciences Museum: An interesting place to explore nature and biodiversity.


Hermitage of San Andrés and forest park of the Valle de los Perales: A natural environment where you can enjoy the beauty of the Sierra de Viso del Marqués


Viso del Marqués is a destination full of history and charm! 🏰🌿

This charming place is located in the south of the province of Ciudad Real, near Andalusia, and is part of the Sierra Morena region of Ciudad Real. 74 kilometres south of the provincial capital, Almuradiel is crossed by the Southern Highway and is located between kilometre points 231 and 243. Near the town, the Despeñaperros Pass begins, a gorge carved by the Despeñaperros River.

Plaza de España and the Parish Church of Santiago el Mayor: the historical and monumental heart of Torrenueva (Ciudad Real).

The Church of Santiago el Mayor is the most prominent monument in Torrenueva and one of the architectural gems of the Campo de Montiel region. It is located in Plaza de España, the town's urban and social center. Its construction began around 1450 and was completed around 1550. It belongs to the Isabelline Gothic style, although it also features Renaissance elements in its ornamentation.
